200.q: Portuguese forward. 5x Ballon d'Or winner. Famous Siuuu celebration. Currently plays for Al-Nassr.
Argentine forward. 8x Ballon d'Or winner. Left-footed dribbler. Lifted the 2022 World Cup.
Brazilian forward. Spent time at Barcelona and PSG. Dazzling skill moves. Wears 10 for his country.
French forward. Won the World Cup at age 19. Plays for Real Madrid. Explosive sprinting speed.
Norwegian striker. Shattered Premier League goal records. Son of a former Leeds defender. Plays for Manchester City.
Egyptian winger. Liverpool's Premier League era top scorer. Left-footed. Known as the Egyptian King.
Belgian playmaker. Manchester City midfielder. Pinpoint crosses and through balls. Famous red hair.
French striker. Scored in the 1998 World Cup final. Arsenal's all-time top scorer. Nicknamed Titi.
English striker. Former Tottenham captain now at Bayern Munich. Three Lions skipper. England's all-time top scorer.
Welsh winger. Four Champions League trophies at Real Madrid. Keen golfer. Scored a bicycle kick in the 2018 final.
Senegalese forward. Premier League and UCL winner at Liverpool. Later moved to Bayern Munich. Africa Cup of Nations winner.
German goalkeeper. Lifted the 2014 World Cup. Sweeper-keeper pioneer. Longtime Bayern Munich number one.
French midfielder. Head-butted Materazzi in the 2006 final. Won three straight UCLs as Real Madrid coach. Elegant playmaker.
Brazilian striker. Won the 2002 World Cup. Scored twice in that final. Known as R9.
Swedish striker. Famous bicycle kick against England. Played for Barca, Milan, and Manchester United. Enormous ego.
English midfielder. Married a Spice Girl. Pinpoint crosses from deep. Wore 7 at Manchester United.
Dutch total-football icon. Won three European Cups with Ajax. Gave his name to a famous turn. Later managed Barcelona.
Italian forward. Roma legend. Nicknamed Il Gladiatore. Spent his entire career at his hometown club.
Argentine striker. Manchester City legend. Scored the 93:20 Premier League winner vs QPR. Former partner of Maradona's daughter.
Spanish goalkeeper. Real Madrid number one for over a decade. Lifted the 2010 World Cup as captain. Nicknamed San Iker.
Croatian midfielder. 2018 Ballon d'Or winner. Six UCL titles at Real Madrid. Small-framed maestro.
Portuguese midfielder. Manchester United captain. Long-range shots and leadership. Known for set-piece deliveries.
Argentine winger. 2022 World Cup winner. Scored in Champions League and Copa America finals. Nicknamed El Fideo.
French defender. 2018 World Cup winner. Former Madrid and Manchester United center-back. Shaved head.
Spanish striker. Real Madrid legend. All-time club top scorer before a Portuguese passed him. Wore 7 at the Bernabeu.
Argentine forward. Two World Cup trophies. Scored the Hand of God and the Goal of the Century. Napoli icon.
Brazilian forward. Three World Cup titles. Scored over 1,200 career goals. Crowned The King.
English midfielder. Liverpool captain. 2005 Istanbul comeback hero. Slipped vs Chelsea in 2014.
English midfielder. Chelsea's all-time top scorer. Played alongside Terry and Cole. Disallowed goal vs Germany in 2010.
Welsh winger. Manchester United legend. Most decorated English league player. Left-footed wing wizard.

400.q: German goal machine. Scored 68 times for West Germany. Nicknamed Der Bomber. 1974 World Cup winner.
Dutch striker. Won Euro 1988 for his country. AC Milan legend. Retired early from ankle injuries.
Liberian striker. 1995 Ballon d'Or winner. Later elected president of his country. Milan icon of the 1990s.
Ivorian midfielder. Barcelona La Liga winner before Manchester City. Powerhouse long-range shots. Three PL titles at City.
Croatian striker. 2018 World Cup finalist. Juventus trophy winner. Classic volley against France in that final.
German midfielder. 2014 World Cup winner. Bayern Munich stalwart. Later married tennis star Ana Ivanovic.
Spanish defender. Euro 2008 and 2010 World Cup winner. Barcelona captain. Long curly hair.
Colombian playmaker. 2014 World Cup Golden Boot. Moved to Real Madrid that summer. Wore number 10.
Hungarian forward. Real Madrid star of the 1950s. Scored 84 international goals. Nicknamed The Galloping Major.
Argentine-Spanish forward. Real Madrid legend. Won five straight European Cups. Nicknamed the Blond Arrow.
Dutch defender. Liverpool captain. Champions League winner in 2019. Ballon d'Or runner-up that year.
Brazilian right-back. Most decorated player in football history. Played for Barcelona and PSG. Samba celebrations.
English striker. 2001 Ballon d'Or winner. Liverpool and Real Madrid. Rode racehorses after retiring.
English striker. Premier League all-time top scorer. Newcastle United legend. Match of the Day pundit.
English midfielder. Manchester United midfielder. Famous red-headed long-range shots. Rarely gave interviews.
Irish midfielder. Manchester United captain. Fiery temperament. Feuded with Mick McCarthy in 2002.
English defender. Manchester United and Leeds. Drug-test ban in 2003. Later a TV pundit.
English defender. Chelsea captain. Slipped taking the 2008 UCL final penalty. Record trophy haul at Stamford Bridge.
English defender. Manchester United right-back. Sky Sports pundit. Brother Phil also played for United.
Italian midfielder. AC Milan's combative enforcer. 2006 World Cup winner. Intimidating glare on the pitch.
Ukrainian striker. 2004 Ballon d'Or winner. Dynamo Kyiv and AC Milan. Missed the decisive penalty in the 2005 UCL final.
Dutch defender. Scored a famous free kick in the 1992 European Cup final. Later Barcelona and Netherlands coach.
Dutch goalkeeper. Manchester United and Ajax. Won Champions Leagues in 1995 and 2008. Towering reach.
Italian striker. 1982 World Cup Golden Boot and Golden Ball. Scored a hat trick vs Brazil that tournament. Died in 2020.
Argentine forward. Long flowing blond hair. Scored vs Brazil in 1990 World Cup last 16. Played alongside Maradona.
Argentine midfielder. Manchester United and Chelsea. Nicknamed La Brujita. Lazio Serie A winner.
Argentine striker. Fiorentina and Roma legend. All-time Argentina top scorer before Messi. Nicknamed Batigol.
Argentine striker. Scored twice in the 2005 UCL final for Milan. Parma, Lazio, Inter, Chelsea. Copa America finalist.
Portuguese playmaker. Fiorentina and AC Milan. Portuguese Golden Generation. Number 10 elegance.
Portuguese striker. Benfica legend. Represented his country at multiple Euros. Known for aerial ability.

600.q: Argentine midfielder. 1978 World Cup winner. Long Tottenham spell. Later managed the Spurs.
Italian defender. Inter Milan center-back. Provoked a famous head-butt in 2006. Iron-willed enforcer.
Argentine goalkeeper. 2022 World Cup winner. Aston Villa number one. Made an infamous celebration with the Golden Glove.
Jamaican-born English winger. Liverpool forward of the 1980s. Rapped on the World in Motion song. Solo goal against Brazil in 1984.
Danish goalkeeper. Manchester United record-breaker. 1992 Euros winner. His son Kasper also keeps goal.
Turkish striker. Galatasaray legend. UEFA Cup winner in 2000. Scored 11 seconds into the 2002 World Cup third-place match.
Japanese midfielder. One of the first Japanese players in Serie A. Roma Scudetto winner. Retired at 29.
Colombian midfielder. Huge blond afro. Three World Cup appearances. Later played in MLS.
Croatian midfielder. AC Milan Serie A winner. Kicked a policeman at Dinamo Zagreb in 1990. Croatia's 1998 World Cup third-place run.
Ghanaian midfielder. Chelsea midfielder. Mourinho called him The Bison. 2012 UCL winner.
Iranian striker. Held the men's international goals record before Ronaldo passed him. Scored 109 for his country. Former national team coach.
Chilean striker. Wore 1+8 at Inter Milan. Real Madrid Pichichi winner. Played at the 1998 World Cup.
Ghanaian-born French defender. 1998 World Cup and Euro 2000 winner. Milan and Chelsea. Nicknamed The Rock.
English striker. Towering 6'7" frame. Robot goal celebration. Liverpool and Spurs spells.
German libero. 1996 Ballon d'Or winner. Euro 96 champion. Later Borussia Dortmund sporting director.
French defender. 1998 World Cup and Euro 2000 winner. Juventus and Parma. His country's most capped player for years.
French forward. 1958 Ballon d'Or winner. Stade de Reims and Real Madrid. Won three straight European Cups.
French midfielder. Three consecutive Ballons d'Or in the 1980s. Euro 1984 top scorer. Later banned from FIFA.
Italian striker. Cagliari legend. His country's all-time top scorer for decades. Scored 35 in 42 internationals.
German striker. 1980 and 1981 Ballon d'Or winner. Bayern Munich forward. Later Bayern CEO.
Italian goalkeeper. 1982 World Cup winner as captain at age 40. Juventus legend. Later national team coach.
Spanish forward. Nicknamed El Buitre. Leader of Real Madrid's Quinta del Buitre. Scored 4 vs Denmark at the 1986 World Cup.
German midfielder. Played for Barcelona, Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid. Euro 1980 winner. Creative playmaker.
English midfielder. Famous tears at Italia 90. Scored a volley against Scotland at Euro 96. Dentist's chair celebration.
Welsh striker. Liverpool's all-time top scorer. FA Cup final goals specialist. Trademark moustache.
Scottish forward. Liverpool player and manager. Nicknamed King. Celtic legend before his Anfield move.
English striker. 1986 World Cup Golden Boot winner. Never booked in his career. Hosts Match of the Day.
Nigerian striker. Played for Ajax, Arsenal and Portsmouth. Olympic gold in 1996. Survived a heart condition scare mid-career.
Nigerian midfielder. Dazzling skills at Bolton Wanderers. Also turned out for PSG and Fenerbahce. Africa Cup of Nations 1994 winner.
Cameroonian striker. Famous corner-flag dance. Oldest World Cup goalscorer at age 42. Italia 90 star.
